---
title: "The Sigmoid Commercial Knowledge Base"
subtitle: "The taxonomy of commercial concepts — the syllabus for everything we teach"
---

**Version:** 0.1 (Draft) · **Status:** Working document · **Owner:** [Name] · **Last updated:** [Date]
**Inherits from:** *The Sigmoid Commercial System* · feeds *The Sigmoid Editorial System*.

---

## What this is

Every idea we teach is a **concept.** This document is the organised list of them — the syllabus behind
all our content, courses, and product explanations.

Two rules make it powerful:

1. **Every concept is a content atom.** One concept becomes an article, a LinkedIn post, a carousel, a
   webinar, a customer guide, a course lesson, a book chapter, *and* a feature explanation.
2. **Every concept has a home in the system.** Concepts are grouped by the Sigmoid Commercial System —
   the five commercial questions and the commercial journey — so nothing is off-topic and every piece
   of content can name its lineage (see *The Sigmoid Editorial System*, §2).

Concepts are grouped **A–H** and numbered, so each has a stable id (e.g. `B-07 Lead Quality`) the
editorial system can track. This is a living taxonomy: the list below is the comprehensive core
(~180 concepts) and is designed to grow toward the full 300–500 as we teach.

---

## A · Foundations — the system itself

*The concepts that describe how commerce works. These frame everything else.*

A-01 Commercial system · A-02 Commercial health · A-03 Commercial maturity ·
A-04 Commercial capability · A-05 The commercial learning loop · A-06 Information vs evidence ·
A-07 Decision quality · A-08 Confidence · A-09 Commercial intelligence · A-10 Evidence over opinion ·
A-11 Leading vs lagging indicators · A-12 Revenue as an outcome of decisions ·
A-13 Relationships as assets · A-14 Learning as a commercial capability · A-15 Commercial execution.

## B · Find customers — Acquire

*How the right customers are attracted.*

B-01 Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) · B-02 Market segmentation · B-03 Targeting · B-04 Positioning ·
B-05 Differentiation · B-06 Value proposition · B-07 Lead quality · B-08 Lead sources ·
B-09 Demand generation · B-10 Inbound · B-11 Outbound / prospecting · B-12 Referrals ·
B-13 Partnerships / channel · B-14 Brand awareness · B-15 Content marketing · B-16 Cost per lead ·
B-17 Marketing-qualified lead (MQL) · B-18 Total addressable market (TAM) · B-19 Buyer personas ·
B-20 Message-market fit.

## C · Win customers — Qualify & Sell

*How opportunities become customers.*

C-01 Lead qualification · C-02 Discovery · C-03 BANT (Budget, Authority, Need, Timing) ·
C-04 MEDDICC · C-05 Sales process · C-06 Sales stages · C-07 Pipeline · C-08 Opportunity ·
C-09 Buying committee / roles · C-10 Decision-maker · C-11 Champion · C-12 Economic buyer ·
C-13 Objection handling · C-14 Discounting · C-15 Proposal · C-16 Negotiation ·
C-17 Conversion / close rate · C-18 Win/loss analysis · C-19 Sales-cycle length · C-20 Deal velocity ·
C-21 Sales-qualified lead (SQL) · C-22 Competitor displacement · C-23 Buying signals ·
C-24 Sales enablement · C-25 Follow-up (in-deal).

## D · Keep customers — Deliver & Retain

*How customers are kept, and how risk is seen early.*

D-01 Onboarding · D-02 Delivery / fulfilment · D-03 Customer experience (CX) · D-04 Customer success ·
D-05 Follow-up discipline · D-06 Communication cadence · D-07 Customer history / context ·
D-08 Relationship continuity · D-09 Engagement · D-10 Customer satisfaction (CSAT) ·
D-11 Net Promoter Score (NPS) · D-12 Complaints handling · D-13 Churn · D-14 Churn prediction ·
D-15 At-risk signals / early warning · D-16 Renewal · D-17 Retention rate · D-18 Win-back / save ·
D-19 Trust · D-20 Service-level expectations.

## E · Grow customers — Grow

*How relationships grow in value over time.*

E-01 Account management · E-02 Upsell · E-03 Cross-sell · E-04 Expansion revenue ·
E-05 Customer segmentation (value tiers) · E-06 Key accounts · E-07 Account planning ·
E-08 Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) · E-09 Net revenue retention (NRR) · E-10 Advocacy ·
E-11 Referral generation · E-12 Whitespace analysis · E-13 Share of wallet · E-14 Loyalty.

## F · Decide better — Measure & Improve

*How the business sees itself and improves its decisions.*

F-01 Commercial metrics · F-02 Pipeline health · F-03 Forecasting · F-04 Forecast accuracy ·
F-05 Revenue predictability · F-06 Dashboards · F-07 Decision frameworks · F-08 Hypotheses ·
F-09 Evidence register · F-10 Validation · F-11 Message / A-B testing · F-12 Attribution ·
F-13 Cohort analysis · F-14 Sensitivity analysis · F-15 Confidence grades · F-16 Provenance ·
F-17 Commercial review cadence · F-18 Benchmarking · F-19 Prioritisation · F-20 Scenario planning.

## G · Data, AI & technology

*The tools that support the system — never replace it.*

G-01 CRM · G-02 Data quality · G-03 Clean customer data · G-04 Single view of the customer ·
G-05 Customer data platform · G-06 Analytics · G-07 Reporting · G-08 Predictive models ·
G-09 AI augmentation · G-10 AI vs automation · G-11 Explainability · G-12 Decision support ·
G-13 Integrations · G-14 Data governance · G-15 Human-in-the-loop.

## H · Themes & beliefs — the angles we own

*Cross-cutting ideas that carry our point of view (our signature ideas live here).*

H-01 Manage customers with evidence, not memory · H-02 Follow-up is a competitive advantage ·
H-03 Every lost customer leaves evidence · H-04 Clean data creates better decisions ·
H-05 Customer relationships are measurable · H-06 AI should strengthen human judgement ·
H-07 Every business is a commercial system · H-08 Commercial Health as a vital sign ·
H-09 The South African commercial reality · H-10 Opinion → evidence.

---

## How each concept becomes content

Every entry above is a reusable atom. The editorial system draws from it and expands it into formats:

| From one concept… | …we can produce |
|---|---|
| e.g. **B-07 Lead Quality** | a LinkedIn post · a carousel · an article · a webinar · a customer guide · a course lesson · a chapter · a product-feature explanation |

That is the leverage: **write the concept once, teach it many ways.** A single well-understood concept
can fuel a month of content across channels — and the same concept explains a product capability to a
buyer.

## Using and growing the taxonomy

- **Tag every piece of content** with its concept id(s). Over time this shows what we've taught, what
  we've over-covered, and the gaps.
- **Add concepts as the market teaches us** — new objections, new questions, new language captured as
  evidence become new entries. The Knowledge Base grows the way the SCS says everything should:
  from evidence.
- **The Academy modules** (Commercial Health, Decisions, Learning, Systems, Intelligence, AI,
  Analytics) are simply clusters of these concepts, sequenced.
